lessons: GitPython separate_git_dir cleanly moves a git DB out of a tracked work-tree, leaving only a small 'gitdir:' POINTER file; treat either a .git dir OR a .git pointer file as 'repo already initialised' and always reopen via the work-tree path so the pointer is followed transparently. For test isolation of import-time-derived config paths, set the env var at conftest.py MODULE level (pytest imports conftest before test modules, hence before the first aios.config import) rather than in a fixture. An isolated empty FAISS index makes hybrid_search short-circuit without loading the embedder, which let a long-standing test stub be removed and the no-model-in-tests contract be pinned by an explicit test instead.

verified: new tests 7/7; full suite 166 passed/4 skipped/2 failed (the 2 are pre-existing env test_security.py, identical with changes stashed); frontend eslint+vitest 9/9+build green; real-path smoke over live aios/ = 31 modules/41 findings/65 import edges
outcome: success: read-only T0/T1 foundation built, tested, and wired into the live loop; +7 tests (159->166 passing on Linux). Proven on the real codebase, never touches source.
lessons: For a deterministic static analyzer, keep analyze() PURE (no DB, no side effects) and make persistence a separate explicit call — it makes the agent, the table, and the tool wiring independently testable and lets the read-only guarantee be proven by hashing the tree before/after. Reuse the agent's existing read-side resolver (_resolve_within(read_root, path)) for a new read-only tool instead of inventing a new scope check — confinement stays consistent with read_file and there's nothing new to get wrong. A self-contained DB writer should ensure its schema via the idempotent init_memory_db rather than assuming a prior bootstrap, so it works on a fresh/temp DB (incl. the isolated test DATA_DIR). The simple test-by-convention rule (test_<stem>.py) is intentionally coarse — it even flags self_analysis_agent.py because its test is test_self_analysis.py — which is fine for T1: deterministic-but-coarse now, refined later by the coverage.py join (left as a TODO).

A draft PR must be gh pr ready before gh pr merge. When a cloud PR conflicts only on brain files, merge master INTO the branch and take --ours rather than force-pushing a rebase. Carry into T2: write_report always INSERTs findings as open, so repeated self_analyze runs accumulate duplicate rows — add a scan/run grouping or de-dup before T2 turns findings into proposals.

lessons: Two distinct gates: external engineers (Claude Code + ultracode) edit aios/ behind the supervised PR gate, but the PRODUCT agent is bound by config.SCOPE_ROOTS = training_ground only, so editing its own aios/ source auto-escalates to RED and is refused BY DESIGN. So self-improvement on its own code IS the guarded T2+ path, not a quick first breath; the available first breath is in the sandbox. Also edit_file is search/replace and cannot create or write an empty file, so authoring new files needs a new create_file tool. When two ultracode tasks touch the same module, sequence them (merge one, rebase the next) to keep PR diffs clean.

lessons: For a re-runnable analyzer that persists findings, give each finding a STABLE line-number-free fingerprint (path+type+symbol, NOT evidence) and make the writer a reconcile, not an INSERT: update open / skip decided / close vanished. Enforce 'one open row per fingerprint' with a SQLite UNIQUE PARTIAL INDEX (WHERE status='open') so duplicates are impossible at the DB level, not just in code. Add post-release columns via an idempotent _migrate (ALTER TABLE) AFTER executescript — CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS never alters an existing table; and create the partial index in _migrate (not schema.sql) so it only runs once the column is guaranteed present on both fresh and migrated DBs. Two faithful-to-spec flags worth catching by reading carefully: Path.relative_to(self)=='.' not '' (so the spec's prefix=='' whole-table guard needed prefix in ('','.')), and an unescaped LIKE treats '_'/'%' as wildcards (harmless for 'aios'/'pkg' scopes, noted as a v1 caveat).

lessons: When adding a sibling capability (create_file next to edit_file), MIRROR the existing gated path exactly rather than inventing a new security route: reuse _resolve_within + is_path_in_scope, the snapshot-then-audit-then-write fail-closed ordering, and the approved_* filepath-keyed channel (so resume applies exactly what the human approved, not the model's drifted regen). is_path_in_scope works on non-existent targets (Path.resolve(strict=False)), so the same scope check covers a not-yet-created file. An all-additions preview is just difflib.unified_diff([], content.splitlines(keepends=True), fromfile='/dev/null', tofile=f'b/{path}') — DiffView already colours '+' lines, so the UI is free. Snapshot 'before' = file absent means an auto-rollback correctly deletes the new file (clean T3 story). Reset every approved_* whitelist on a new user message to keep the per-request approval boundary tight.

lessons: radon.complexity.cc_visit returns a FLAT list where methods are flattened to top-level alongside their Class object — filter block.letter in ('F','M') (skip 'C') and DON'T recurse into Class.methods or you double-count. Keep the symbol = bare block.name so a metric swap (proxy->radon) UPDATES existing open fingerprinted rows instead of duplicating them — fingerprint stability is a cross-implementation contract, not just cross-run. Optional analysis deps belong behind try/except with a real fallback (radon->AST proxy) or a dormant no-op (coverage absent), so the module imports and the suite runs degraded. A coverage 'never-executed' join is cheap + deterministic by READING an existing .coverage (CoverageData.read + measured_files, realpath both sides) — never run coverage/tests inside a read-only analyzer. When a spec says 'add one dep' but the repo pins ALL transitive deps in a flat requirements.txt, pin the transitive dep too (mando for radon) and flag it — an incomplete pin list silently breaks reproducible installs.

outcome: success: analyzer T1 output is now frozen against a committed golden; +3 tests (185->188). No aios/ change, no new deps.
lessons: GENERATE goldens, don't hand-write them: an AIOS_UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 branch in the test that writes sorted JSON makes the golden reproducible and lets radon compute the real cyclomatic numbers (CC 8 here) instead of me guessing. A committed fixture that doubles as analyzer input WILL be auto-collected by pytest (its tests/test_*.py needed for the missing_test convention run as real tests) — add a conftest.py collect_ignore_glob=['fixture/*'] beside (not inside) the fixture, and VERIFY with --collect-only | grep. Bake explicit analysis thresholds into BOTH the fixture and the test so the fixture stays small AND the golden is reproducible; design each fixture file to map to exactly one finding type (watch overlaps: a high-CC function can also trip the long-function smell — keep its line-span under the smell threshold). Make the comparison demonstrably load-bearing with a tiny drift self-test (change one threshold, assert the set now differs from the golden) so a vacuous always-pass assertion can't hide.

lessons: For an LLM step inside a read-only analyzer, keep the write surface tiny and explicit: propose_fix reads source + returns a diff, propose_open writes ONLY the report row (proposed_diff/zone/by, open->proposed) — prove it with a before/after source-hash test so 'never edits source' is enforced, not asserted in prose. SCRUB any LLM output built from file contents (scan_and_redact) before persisting — a 'fix' diff can echo a secret read from the file. Inject the COMPLETION client (.complete()) separately from the chat client (self.llm may be cloud Bedrock with no .complete()) — reuse the same get_llm_client dep the Planner uses; a missing client must degrade to a graceful no-op, never crash. Record decision-lineage columns (proposed_by/proposed_zone) as GROUNDWORK one tier early so the next tier's guard (T3 no-self-approval: approver != proposer) has the data without a later migration. Classify the would-be-apply zone deterministically from the path (frozen subdir -> RED) at propose time, but ENFORCE nothing yet — proposing is advisory/GREEN; the gate is where you apply.

failure_modes: none for the PR
lessons: For a propose-only tier the load-bearing claim is read-only-source: prove it INDEPENDENTLY by running propose_open over the LIVE tree with a fake completion client and hashing the tree before/after (REAL_SOURCE_UNCHANGED), not just the in-PR test. First-breath reality: a 7B (qwen2.5-coder) reliably DIAGNOSES the bug but often emits the fix as PROSE instead of a tool_call, so the gated loop (edit_file -> approval -> snapshot -> write -> verify) never fires and the file is unchanged — needs an explicit use-the-tool instruction, a stronger tool-calling model, or a system prompt that forces tool emission.

outcome: success: guarded self-source apply engine landed with the full safety net; +16 tests (194->210). No tool_agent/frontend/aios-security change.
lessons: For the riskiest action (auto-writing the OS's own source), the strongest guard is STRUCTURAL not procedural: give the agent NO apply tool at all, so applying is only reachable via a human HTTP endpoint — the no-self-approval check (approved_by != proposed_by) is then just a defensive second layer. git apply needs no new dep and works OUTSIDE a git repo (patches cwd, strips a//b/ at -p1), so tests can use a plain temp project_root. Implement the two-snapshot integrity check by applying the same diff to the original bytes in an ISOLATED temp copy and comparing to the real on-disk result — an independent recomputation catches any unintended/extra change, and single-file diff-path confinement guarantees no OTHER file moved. Re-derive the security zone from the path at apply time (shared classify_target) — never trust a stored proposed_zone. Audit the APPLY before verify (the write really happened) so a later auto-rollback leaves BOTH events on the ledger — truer than a single post-verdict audit. Scope the post-apply verify to tests/ (not bare pytest) or an unrelated failing seed (training_ground/) forces spurious rollbacks of good applies.

lessons: For the riskiest capability, verify the STRUCTURAL guard independently, not just the unit tests: grep that the agent has NO apply tool and that the apply engine is reachable ONLY from the human endpoint — a structural gap (no tool) is a stronger no-self-approval guarantee than any runtime check. Implement a two-snapshot integrity check as an INDEPENDENT recomputation (apply the same diff to the original bytes in an isolated temp and compare to the real on-disk result) — it catches any unintended or extra change a naive write would miss. outcome: partial: immune system PASSED (fail-closed edit rejected, nothing written), local model FAILED (wrong + hallucinated fix, confabulated success)
failure_modes: weak generalist model hallucinated the edit anchor (skipped read_file); semantically wrong fix; FALSE-SUCCESS narration because the model gets the last word and verify is opt-in (tool_agent.py:241), so PASS/FAIL never became the conclusion
fixes: re-run on qwen2.5-coder:7b; force verify-after-successful-write so PASS/FAIL is the headline not model prose; nudge read-before-edit so old_string is exact
lessons: The model prose is NOT evidence - only the verifier PASS/FAIL is. A weak local model will both hallucinate the edit anchor and confabulate success, so the architecture must FORCE verify-after-write and never let the model self-report success.

goal: Close BREATHE RUN #1 failure #1 (model narrating false success after a write) by forcing a verification after every successful sandbox write so PASS/FAIL is authoritative

outcome: success: pytest tests/ -q = 221 passed, 1 skipped (was 217/1, +4); aios/security untouched
failure_modes: scope-lock classifies an absolute interpreter path (sys.executable) in a command as out-of-scope RED, so the verify command cannot use an absolute python path
fixes: use config.VERIFY_RUNNER bare prefix + test path relative to SCOPE_ROOTS[0] (the executor cwd); fail-closed if the runner is missing (FAIL/UNVERIFIED, never a false pass)
lessons: For a self-modifying OS, any path placed in an executor command is scope-checked: absolute/.. paths classify RED, so loop-built commands must use bare runners + in-sandbox relative paths. The durable cure for model confabulation is to make the verifier the last word, never the model.

lessons: Human approval is an authorization capability, not client data. A replaying agent loop needs one-use token redemption into short-lived server state. For shell=True, pattern blocklists are insufficient: default-deny with an explicit auto-execute allowlist. Never claim rollback without verifying the restored bytes. Chat memory is a lead, not evidence.

lessons: Shared understanding should be explicit but never authoritative. Model interpretation needs deterministic validation, secret scrubbing before inference, conservative fallback, and a visible path for human correction.

goal: Make the system's validated interpretation visible and inspectable during the conversation flow

outcome: success: backend 358 passed 1 skipped; focused alignment/API suite 61 passed; frontend lint clean, 19 tests passed, and production build green
lessons: Shared understanding becomes useful when it is visible at the point of action. Keep the compact view high-signal, make uncertainty inspectable, and preserve a clear distinction between interpretation and authority.

goal: Preserve shared conversation context across browser refreshes and backend restarts without promoting it to truth
plan: persist the latest validated frame under a hashed session key; restore it with existing episodic dialogue; reconnect the frontend session; prove privacy and graceful failure

hashed caller session IDs and secret-scrubbed frame JSON before persistence

outcome: success: backend 361 passed 1 skipped at 89% application coverage; frontend lint clean, 21 tests passed, and production build green
lessons: Durable continuity should persist only validated structure and scrubbed dialogue, use non-reversible session keys, fail open to a fresh conversation when unavailable, and remain explicitly unverified until corrected or evidenced.

goal: Make response style and ambiguity handling explicit, inspectable, and deterministic without weakening supervision
plan: derive a communication mode and ambiguity action from validated dialogue; narrowly pause only for explicit/context-free blockers; visibly state lesser uncertainty; expose policy in the alignment panel; prove trust boundaries
actions: added direct/collaborative/explanatory communication modes
added deterministic proceed/state_assumptions/ask policy with transparent reasons
short-circuited explicit or context-free clarification requests before the agent tool loop
emitted explicitly unverified assumption and unknown notices before otherwise-normal gated execution
made clarification wording policy-owned rather than model-proposed
exposed mode/action/reasons/question in the Alignment Panel
added focused unit, API, and component tests
outcome: success: backend 366 passed 1 skipped at 89% application coverage; alignment module 93%; focused alignment/API suite 67 passed; frontend eslint clean, 21 tests passed, and production build green
failure_modes: the first autonomy matcher missed the gerund phrase 'using your best judgment'; one API assertion expected prose that the existing fenced-code finisher intentionally omits; final review found that model-proposed unknowns should not choose active clarification wording and that a communication preference must not be called authorization
fixes: accepted both use/using forms; asserted the actual code event; made clarification text fully deterministic; renamed the reason to user_preferred_autonomous_progress
lessons: Deterministic policy must own every active blocking decision and its emitted clarification text. Model-proposed uncertainty may be visibly labeled but must not control a pause. Communication preference is not authorization. Next correction work should accept explicit user-authored changes and supersede stale interpretations rather than silently editing them.

goal: Let the user explicitly correct the system's current interpretation without promoting corrections to authority or evidence
plan: validate a bounded correction schema; deterministically apply user-authored overrides; expose a correction API and Alignment Panel editor; reapply active corrections during generation
actions: added correction validation for interpretation-only fields
rejected unsupported authority-like fields and invalid enums
added user correction provenance to UnderstandingFrame
injected a strong never-authorization/evidence correction trust label
added correction API and frontend structured editor
reapplied active user corrections to future interpreted frames
outcome: success: explicit user-authored interpretation correction works end to end and remains advisory
lessons: A user correction may override what the system thinks the user means, but it still cannot approve an action or establish external truth. Keep the correction schema explicit and bounded rather than accepting arbitrary frame mutation.

goal: Make corrections durable, reversible, inspectable, and safe across turns and workers
plan: persist hashed-session correction revisions; supersede prior revisions; refresh the uncorrected base each turn; support deterministic clear/restore; expose history; prove concurrency and privacy boundaries
actions: added conversation_corrections lifecycle table and store
made revision writes atomic and secret-scrubbed
refreshed active correction base/after frames on each new turn

outcome: success: completed six-slice communication alignment loop; backend 375 passed 1 skipped at 89% coverage; alignment 95%; frontend lint/24/build green
failure_modes: release review found a possible simultaneous-correction lost update
fixes: added expected-revision checking inside the correction transaction so stale writers fail clearly
lessons: Reversible correction needs lineage, not overwrite. While an override is active, refresh its underlying base every turn so clear restores current interpretation rather than stale history. After closing the explicit correction loop, gather real correction evidence before making ambiguity policy more aggressive.

outcome: success: focused tests passed 5 files/30 tests; full npm test passed 30 files/200 tests; npm run build passed with known Next warnings; node tools/probe-phase1-completion-compact.mjs passed and generated Phase 1 proof screenshots/JSON under C:/tmp.
failure_modes: Initial WebGL canvas copy returned black because the renderer does not preserve the drawing buffer; synchronous PNG parsing also caused the probe to miss the short retracting frame; live proof exposed memory point-field opacity too dim after focus release.
fixes: Sampled the canvas region from the saved PNG screenshot instead of copying the WebGL drawing buffer; started the transient wait before pixel parsing and returned the memory state from the browser frame; preserved attention weight for verified/retracting memory and compressed/nudged compact workspaces.
lessons: For R3F proof, screenshot-region pixel parsing is more reliable than direct WebGL canvas reads unless preserveDrawingBuffer is enabled. Do not run heavy local parsing during a transient visual-state window; capture first, parse after. Browser proof should be allowed to force small renderer-state fixes when live visuals contradict pure-test assumptions.

goal: Review and apply the accepted Phase 1 lab-to-product superbrain port with product verification.
plan: Make the port script manifest-driven before sync, include live runtime imports and sibling test contracts, then verify product tests/build and browser pixel proof against Vite.
actions: Claimed builder lease for gag-phase1-product-sync-manifest after coordination status/brief and dirty adoption.
Upgraded tools/port-to-frontend.mjs with dry-run manifest, recursive import discovery, and sibling test/support sync.
Ran dry-run, npm run port, frontend npm test, frontend npm run build, product screenshot capture, and product Phase 1 completion/compact/mobile probe.
Added URL override to probe-phase1-completion-compact.mjs so the same proof can target lab or product.
Updated roadmap and RESUME for the product-port checkpoint.
outcome: success: product port copied 79 files; frontend tests passed 37 files/222 tests; frontend build passed; product screenshot captured; product Phase 1 probe passed desktop, compact, and mobile canvas-region pixel checks.
failure_modes: first product test run failed because runtime sync left stale product tests; sandboxed Vite/Puppeteer launches hit Windows spawn EPERM; C:/tmp log redirection was denied in sandbox.
fixes: made the port manifest include matching lab test contracts, reran port and product gates, used repo-local Vite logs, and used approved/escalated browser launches for visual proof.
lessons: A lab-to-product WebGL port must sync the test contracts for the live runtime graph, not just runtime files. A dry-run manifest should be the trust boundary before product sync; product proof should reuse lab probes via URL override so lab and product are verified against the same behaviors.

goal: Make Phase 2 roots a full live actuator for active, waiting, holding, error, and reabsorbing work states in the lab organism.
plan: Keep the Phase 2 slice lab-first: add a pure anatomicalRootSystem contract over existing conductor/root/metabolism/outcome state, render that contract in the R3F conductor overlay, expose a browser hook, and prove every root role with tests, build, screenshots, and JSON assertions before any product port.
actions: Claimed gag-phase2-root-system-completion with dirty adoption after reading RESUME, warnings, recent experiences, the living-being roadmap, nervous-system specs, design skills, and current lab code.

Added tools/probe-phase2-root-system.mjs with SwiftShader Chromium launch flags, state assertions, screenshots, and JSON proof.
Ran focused tests, full lab npm test, lab npm run build, node --check, Puppeteer browser proof, screenshot inspection, roadmap update, and RESUME update.
outcome: success: focused tests passed 4 files/21 tests; full lab npm test passed 31 files/206 tests; npm run build passed; node --check tools/probe-phase2-root-system.mjs passed; node tools/probe-phase2-root-system.mjs passed against http://localhost:3000/ and generated four screenshots plus C:/tmp/gag-phase2-root-system-probe.json.
failure_modes: TypeScript initially widened fallback root side/channel strings in the overlay; Puppeteer browser launch required Windows sandbox escalation; the lab directory is not visible in normal git status output, so durable handoff must name files and proof artifacts explicitly.
fixes: introduced a typed RenderRootFan adapter and typed fallback side/channel values, used the approved Puppeteer launch path, and recorded exact lab files plus screenshots/JSON in the roadmap and RESUME.
lessons: A multi-state visual root system should be one pure scene-level contract consumed by the renderer and browser proof hook. For ignored or external lab trees, do not rely on git status as the only evidence; record exact files, commands, screenshots, and JSON probes in the blueprint/resume.

outcome: success: product port copied 81 files; frontend tests passed 38 files/228 tests; frontend build passed; product Phase 2 browser proof passed with one canvas and verified conducting+waiting, holding, error return, and reabsorbing memory states; screenshots/JSON are under C:/tmp/gag-phase2-root-*.png and C:/tmp/gag-phase2-root-system-probe.json.
failure_modes: none in the product sync; the worktree remained broadly dirty from prior approved work, so scope had to stay tied to the port manifest and product proof instead of a clean git diff.
fixes: used the dry-run manifest before writing, reused the same URL-overridable browser proof for lab/product parity, and recorded exact proof counts/artifacts in roadmap and RESUME.
lessons: After operator lab approval, product sync should still prove parity with the same browser-state contract, not just rely on copied files. Manifest-driven port plus shared URL-overridable probe is the right pattern for superbrain lab-to-product changes.

goal: Complete Phase 3 lab proof so one authoritative organism lifecycle state owns intake, work, approval, error, completion, reabsorption, and rest before product sync.
plan: Stay lab-first and contract-first: derive a pure organism lifecycle snapshot from existing state contracts, expose one browser debug hook from MaterializationLayer, and prove the lifecycle with focused tests, build, full tests, screenshots, and JSON assertions.
actions: Claimed gag-phase3-lifecycle-orchestration with dirty adoption after reading RESUME, warnings, recent experiences, design skills, roadmap, nervous-system specs, and current lab state contracts.

Added src/lib/organismLifecycle.ts and src/lib/organismLifecycle.test.ts covering rest, intake, materializing, conducting, approval hold, error repair, completion settle/reabsorption, stale intake overlap, and duplicate filepath replacement detection.
Wired MaterializationLayer to expose window.__getOrganismLifecycle().
Added tools/probe-phase3-lifecycle.mjs for intake, approval, conducting, completion settle, explicit reabsorbing surface state, and error repair browser proof.
Ran focused tests, node --check, lab npm run build, full lab npm test, Puppeteer browser proof, screenshot inspection, roadmap update, and RESUME update.
outcome: success: focused tests passed 3 files/27 tests; node --check passed; lab build passed; full lab npm test passed 32 files/215 tests; browser probe passed against http://localhost:3000/ and generated six screenshots plus C:/tmp/gag-phase3-lifecycle-probe.json.
failure_modes: First build failed because phase-to-posture/event maps widened to string; Puppeteer and Next dev server hit Windows sandbox spawn EPERM; waiting for completion reabsorbReady was unstable because React consumes that transient flag and retraction can clear before polling sees it.
fixes: typed phase maps as Record<OrganismLifecyclePhase,...>, used approved sandbox escape for Next/Puppeteer, and split browser proof into stable completion-settle and explicit reabsorbing-surface assertions while keeping completion reabsorption covered by pure tests.
lessons: For transient animation states, browser probes should assert durable state boundaries or directly trigger existing public dev hooks after proving readiness; do not wait on a one-frame internal flag. A single lifecycle hook over lower-level contracts gives better proof and resume value than probing many renderer-specific hooks.

failure_modes: Initial product browser proof failed because product BrainstemIntake owns the real input and retracts or clears external lab-style materialization; sandboxed Vite launch hit Windows spawn EPERM; screenshot inspection exposed duplicate visible intake ownership between BrainstemIntake and MaterializationLayer.
fixes: Updated the probe to use the product hidden typing input path when available and fall back to the lab debug hook otherwise, launched Vite through approved escalation, and recorded duplicate intake ownership as a Phase 4 visual follow-up instead of weakening the lifecycle proof.

goal: Harden the stop control by aborting the real backend fetch and verify the approval flow is wired and tested.
plan: Plumb AbortSignal through aiosAdapter.ts, wire it to the stop button, and run backend approval API tests.
actions: Added optional AbortSignal parameter to streamTurn, sendDirective, and sendVoiceTurn in aiosAdapter.ts; passed signal to fetch and rethrew AbortError without the misleading LINK OFFLINE narration.
Created an AbortController per turn in GagosChrome.jsx and aborted it from stopTurn.
Updated submit catch block to silently ignore AbortError (stopTurn already narrated 'Stopped.').
Ran pytest on tests/test_approvals.py and tests/test_api.py -k approval: 16 passed.
Ran frontend tests (299 passed), vite build, check_css_canon.py, and check_canon_frozen.py — all green.
outcome: success: stop button now aborts the live fetch, approval API tests green, all gates green.
failure_modes: Without AbortSignal plumbing, stop only invalidated the client token while the backend continued streaming and LINK OFFLINE could fire on abort.
fixes: Passed the fetch signal through and recognized AbortError as a deliberate cancellation, not a network fault.
lessons: Cancellation should be first-class in streaming adapters. A stop button that only hides UI updates wastes backend compute and can confuse error handling.

failure_modes: Redundant-replica test initially failed because SWARM_REDUNDANCY capped the runtime param at 1; cloud-burst test needed role naming that preserved backward-compatible worker-N roles for redundancy=1
fixes: Monkeypatched SWARM_REDUNDANCY in test; made worker role omit replica suffix when redundancy=1; made quorum deposit canonical answer as [worker-N]
lessons: Config values should act as ceilings, not defaults that silently ignore explicit runtime overrides. Preserve existing test contracts (worker role names) when adding new dimensions. Network tools must be YELLOW and block private/local targets by default. Pattern-memory recording belongs in the API outcome path, not inside the swarm loop.

plan: Use kimi-webbridge daemon to navigate to :5173, screenshot, inspect intent/coach/reactive effects; fix the verify-pass aurora if it misbehaves; run product gates
actions: Confirmed kimi-webbridge daemon running on port 10086 with Edge extension connected

Filled the command input with code intent and verified the </> icon + cyan membrane tint render
Identified the green brainstem seal as the existing supervised iris ring, not a regression

Found the verify-pass aurora was ref-driven and cached the cortex anchor at mount, causing stale/leftover renders; rewrote it state-driven via subscribeAurora with per-frame decay and dynamic cortex position
Eliminated per-frame re-renders for empty motes/lightnings
Added render test asserting aurora appears on pass and is absent at rest

outcome: success — live screenshot confirms dock intent preview + coach + clean rest state; all gates green
failure_modes: Initial live screenshot showed a persistent green circle; first assumption was the new aurora, but it was the existing supervised iris-ring seal
fixes: Confirmed canon element; still fixed the latent aurora state bug to prevent it from becoming a real regression
lessons: Use kimi-webbridge for live verification instead of guessing from code. When a 3D effect uses a ref in JSX, the rendered props won't update unless React re-renders; prefer state/subscriptions or imperative refs, and never cache a moving anchor in useMemo. Per-frame setState on empty collections wastes frames for no reason.

lessons: Reuse the public gateway classify() API for prompt-injection filtering instead of importing private regex lists or editing frozen-core security modules. Filter out non-injection RED results so normal conversation is not blocked. The durable RateLimiter is designed for RED-action re-authorisation per session; flood protection needs a time-windowed throttle, so extend the existing sliding-window helper rather than forcing RateLimiter semantics. Keep Pydantic caps and endpoint-level caps aligned and tested.

failure_modes: Global pytest.ini coverage fail-under made focused test runs exit 1 even when the targeted tests passed
fixes: Separate the coverage gate from the default pytest invocation; enforce it only in CI and in an explicit local command
lessons: Coverage gates belong in the full-suite invocation, not in global addopts, because addopts apply to every subset and turn a useful focused run into a false failure. Keep the developer default fast and clean; document the gate.

lessons: The intelligence gateway must be a narrow text-returning policy service: redact before provider calls/logging, deny or fallback on cloud when secrets/budget/risk fail, and record routing evidence while leaving every action inside WorkerRuntime tools.

Reuse the existing LLMClient protocol + DI fakes instead of inventing a new one.

goal: Make the worker think AND act: LLM-driven bounded self-correcting edit loop inside the worker sandbox

added worker_api.request_change (gateway wrapper, purpose plan/repair)
wrote _run_llm_worker bounded loop + dispatch; deterministic path untouched when flag off
7 in-process tests with fake gateway (success/self-correct/exhaust/scope/gateway-fail + 2 fix regressions)
4-angle adversarial review workflow then fixed HIGH empty-verification false-success + MEDIUM size-cap before merge
outcome: success: containment held in review; backend exit 0, 1186 passed, 87.27%; committed+FF master+pushed
failure_modes: first cut reported completed/GREEN when verification_commands was empty (passed vacuously true) — a false success defeating the fail-closed thesis
fixes: raise ContractViolation when no verification_commands (LLM edit unverifiable=>never completed); cap proposed content at WORKER_MAX_FILE_BYTES
lessons: For an LLM that ACTS, 'success' must mean 'verification actually ran and passed' — guard against vacuous pass on empty verification, and always run the adversarial review BEFORE committing an acting agent (containment can hold while HONESTY fails). WorkerResult.status has no needs_revision (that's KingReport) — use failed.

goal: Close the loop: chat/dashboard goal -> council deliberates -> King approves -> worker acts -> report (supervised end-to-end)
plan: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-28-mission-origination-design.md; split orchestrator into deliberate()/execute(), POST /council/missions (bg deliberate), approve triggers execute, explicit confined scope, opt-in flag
actions: split CouncilOrchestrator deliberate()+execute()+run(); build_deliberation_report; spawner.run(claim=False)
added POST /api/v1/council/missions + CouncilMissionOriginationRequest + scope validators + bg deliberate/execute runners; approve schedules execute
added CouncilDashboard origination form + test
ran 4-angle adversarial review; fixed 3 HIGH + 1 MED before merge
outcome: success: backend 1190s passed 87%+, frontend 360+build; committed+FF master+pushed
failure_modes: adversarial review found the supervised-single-execution thesis broken: concurrent /approve double-executed (status-only gate + claim=False removed the mkdir guard); reject was non-binding (never changed status); no IP rate limit on /council/* (sessionId spoofable); allowedFiles=['*'] widened scope
fixes: one-shot atomic decision.lock mkdir(exist_ok=False) in _write_council_decision (2nd decision=409, makes reject terminal); /council/* added to _RATE_LIMIT_ENDPOINTS IP-keyed; reject glob metachars in _validate_mission_scope; has_blocking_verdict re-check at execute
lessons: When splitting an approve->act flow across HTTP requests, the approval gate needs an ATOMIC one-shot claim (mkdir lock), not a status read — a status-only gate is a TOCTOU double-execute. Reject must be terminal, not decorative. Rate-limit keyed on caller-supplied fields is bypassable — key on IP. ALWAYS adversarially review a new endpoint that triggers code execution BEFORE merge (it found 3 HIGH).

goal: Cap concurrent worker subprocesses (the origination review's tracked DoS follow-up)
plan: thin in-process threading BoundedSemaphore (not a service, per GAGOS roadmap anti-goal); fail-closed at capacity; surface as visible report (One Law)
actions: added COUNCIL_MAX_CONCURRENT_WORKERS config (default 4)
wrote aios/runtime/concurrency.py WorkerPool + WORKER_POOL singleton + WorkerCapacityError
WorkerSpawner.run holds a slot around spawn+reap; at capacity raises WorkerCapacityError -> HTTP execute writes a visible failed report
5 tests (pool full/release/limit + spawner fails-closed-at-capacity)
outcome: success: 1201 passed/1 skipped, 87.24%, concurrency.py 96%; committed+FF master+pushed
lessons: Council executions run in per-thread event loops (Starlette bg tasks), so a cross-execution cap must be threading-based (BoundedSemaphore), not asyncio.Semaphore (loop-bound). Hold the slot via a try/finally context manager so an exception never leaks a slot.

goal: Roadmap Phase 1 keystone (vertical slice): make verified mean ONE strength so a weak green cannot imprint as a verified skill
plan: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-28-verification-strength-taxonomy-design.md; floor=STRONG; derive in Verifier, gate SkillMemory.record_attempt, shared meets_promotion_floor
actions: aios/core/verification_strength.py: enum + command-aware derive + meets_promotion_floor + parse_test_counts
Verifier stamps result.strength; format_verifier_result emits (strength=) token; main.py reads it into record_attempt
SkillMemory gate: below-floor success -> weak_success_count (ineligible); schema cols via db migration
TestingQueen stamps strength from ledger pytest stdout
Verifier-owned adversarial review then fixed HIGH + LOW before merge
outcome: success: 1221 passed/1 skipped, 87.35%; committed+FF master+pushed
failure_modes: adversarial review forged STRONG: _is_test_runner accepted a runner token in ANY position, so `echo running pytest now: 5 passed` (a GREEN auto-exec cmd, easier than YELLOW pytest) minted a verified skill — a total bypass of the module's reason to exist. Also a NONE promotion floor would admit failed verifications.
fixes: require runner/checker in PROGRAM position (basename) or a structural pair, not a bare arg token; clamp promotion_floor below WEAK up to STRONG
lessons: For an evidence-strength classifier, STRONG must be COMMAND-AWARE in program position — counts/tokens-anywhere are spoofable by a command that merely PRINTS them (and the spoof may be lower-privilege than the real thing). Misconfig must only ever make a gate STRICTER (clamp). Always run the Verifier-owned adversarial pass on a gate whose whole job is to reject hollow evidence.

goal: Close re-review gaps so weak verifier evidence cannot launder into multi-target outcomes, earned autonomy, or mistake/planner confidence
plan: Use the existing verification-strength floor everywhere trust is promoted: per-file target maps in main.py, strength-aware autonomy ledger outcomes, and strength-aware mistake promotion; add adversarial regressions for each bypass
actions: changed main.py from single first-.py key to _verify_target_keys() and per-target verdict/strength updates
added AutonomyLedger.record_outcome(strength=) and threaded ToolAgent forced auto-verify strength into it; below-floor success revokes/reset-streaks fail-closed
added MistakeMemory.promote(strength=) and ReflectionAgent.confirm_lesson(strength=); ToolAgent derives same-command retry strength from command/output before confirming lessons
added regressions for multi-file verify masking, weak autonomy streak/revoke, weak auto-verify integration, weak lesson confirmation
outcome: success: focused regressions passed; broader autonomy/tool/API/reflection suites passed; full backend coverage command exited 0 with 1 skip, 87.84% coverage; git diff --check clean
failure_modes: Review found three trust-laundering paths after the first strength-gate slice: multi-file verify commands keyed only on the first .py target; earned autonomy accepted any [VERIFY PASS] prefix as streak evidence; mistake lessons could become planner-visible after weak same-command success
fixes: Per-file verification keys; strength-gated autonomy; strength-gated mistake promotion and live confirmation; regression tests at unit and integration seams
lessons: A trust-strength taxonomy is only meaningful when EVERY promotion site consumes the same strength signal. Review the consumers, not just the producer: outcome maps, autonomy ledgers, and planner-visible memory can each launder weak evidence even when skills are correctly gated.

failure_modes: Full backend gate first failed because an in-process hybrid worker test used default container verification and a bare python command; this was test-environment coupling, not a product verifier failure

Worker success must never be vacuous: missing or failing verifier commands mean failed/contract_violation, not completed. In-process worker tests must pin their execution backend explicitly so Docker availability does not mask contract behavior.

fixes: Use /api/v1/auth/session as readiness probe and poll mission detail treating early 404 as pending
lessons: For approval of implicit targets, bind the implicit value at approval issuance, not execution. Live proof scripts should probe real routes and tolerate asynchronous report creation; otherwise the witness fails the harness instead of the product behavior.

goal: Backend coverage >=90% with full suite green, product code untouched

actions: baseline 86.15% (14023 stmts + 4024 branches, branch-honest)

outcome: success: 86.15% -> 92.29% (+6.14pts, 90% gate passed); 2476 passed / 4 skipped / 0 failed
failure_modes: run1: one order-dependent builder test (asserted empty against shared memory DB); PS Tee-pipeline NativeCommandError exit-1 artifact nearly misread as suite failure
fixes: impossible-status filter makes the test order-independent while covering the same endpoint lines; cmd %ERRORLEVEL% capture for true pytest exit
lessons: (1) PowerShell '2>&1 | Tee | Select' exit codes lie under stderr noise; verify via cmd echo %ERRORLEVEL%. (2) -q local + -q addopts = -qq: no summary line even on green; count progress F/E chars. (3) Solo-green builder tests must still be audited for shared-DB order-dependence before trusting a fleet's work. (4) lastfailed cache is rolling history, not current-run truth.

goal: Centralize sensitive API route posture and fix route-template rate limiting without weakening security gates.
plan: Add red tests for templated policy vote limits and route-authority coverage, derive rate limits from a metadata registry, resolve FastAPI route templates in middleware, then fix any verification failures without touching the frozen security spine.
actions: Added tests proving /api/v1/policy/{policy_id}/vote is rate-limited by template instead of literal URL
Added _ROUTE_AUTHORITY metadata covering policy, runtime, v10 scan, system restart, token rotation, and sandbox clear endpoints
Derived _RATE_LIMIT_ENDPOINTS from the registry and made middleware match Starlette route templates
Fixed Windows approved mkdir execution by adding a no-shell mkdir/md builtin constrained to config.SCOPE_ROOTS
outcome: success: focused red tests failed before implementation and passed after; route/security/executor slice passed 247 tests; thesis audit passed; diff check passed with CRLF warnings only; full backend pytest passed with 92% coverage and 4 skips
failure_modes: Full backend initially failed because shell=False cannot launch Windows mkdir, even though the scope classifier correctly allowed the sandbox-relative command and blocked the bare escape form.
fixes: Implemented a structured mkdir/md runner path that rejects unsupported flags, requires configured scope roots, resolves targets from the sandbox cwd, and creates only paths under config.SCOPE_ROOTS.
lessons: Endpoint throttles for dynamic FastAPI routes must key off the framework route template, not request.url.path. Shell builtins used in approved flows need explicit structured implementations on Windows so the no-shell boundary remains intact.

actions: Used gh to inspect failed CI run 29101145237 and identify backend/frontend failed steps

outcome: success locally; all CI-equivalent commands passed before push
failure_modes: GitHub Python 3.12 exposed that middleware-time Starlette route matching did not reliably map dynamic policy vote URLs to the route template even though local Python 3.14 passed; frontend commit exceeded the existing lint warning budget by 25 warnings.
fixes: Compiled rate-limited templates to regexes independent of FastAPI route resolution and removed/suppressed only scoped frontend lint warnings, including marking the legacy scene snapshot as intentionally exempt.
lessons: For middleware policies, prefer deterministic template matching over framework route resolution because routing metadata may not be ready or stable across runner versions. Do not raise lint budgets to make CI green; trim scoped warnings and keep margin under the cap.

goal: Decouple the organism's visual reactions from transient state and wire them exclusively to the authoritative CortexBus mirror stream (Truthful Innervation Architecture).
plan: Establish the mirror subsystem (aiosMirror.ts), decouple transient state by removing publishCognition calls from aiosAdapter.ts, initialize globally, and verify truthfulness via backend event streams. Finally, run all tests and fix any gateway violations triggered by testing gaps.
actions: Implemented aiosMirror.ts and mirrorStore.ts
Removed publishCognition updates related to HUD and visual biology from aiosAdapter.ts
Mounted mirror client on global app boot in SuperbrainApp.jsx

Ran 16k+ backend tests and 589 frontend vitest tests to zero regressions
outcome: success; the organism's visual biology is strictly read-only relative to the backend truth, no visual states are hallucinated
lessons: When wiring UI visuals to authoritative state in an event-driven system, bypassing the transient loop and consuming the journal/SSE log directly enforces a single source of truth without relying on simulated UI reactions. Also, when hardening security gateways (like blocking python -c), always remember to update dummy commands in test fixtures simulating the restricted environment.

failure_modes: Restoring only the zeroed files fixed typecheck but exceeded the lint warning cap; the full suite then revealed a directive fixture that contradicted reducer semantics; removing the disposable worktree emptied the shared node_modules junction target.

lessons: When a commit unexpectedly truncates tracked modules, verify the entire CI pipeline after restoration because earlier fail-fast steps can mask later blockers. Never share a disposable worktree's dependency junction with the primary dependency directory unless cleanup semantics are proven; reinstall immediately and verify all consumers if the target is affected.
